⭐ Task  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 27 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-93  💭 0 comments  🧵 0 linked                          
                                                                                                                      
  # Architecture: flatten the cache hierarchy                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sat, 27 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  ExtractVibeCacheService (10 lines) exists solely to pass a directory name to JsonFileCache .                        
  OutfitAvatarCacheService (45 lines) adds only a stateless buildKey() helper. JsonFileCache is a 74-line abstract    
  base. Three files for what is really two configurations of one cache.                                               
                                                                                                                      
  **Files:**                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  • backend/src/common/cache/json-file-cache.service.ts — abstract class JsonFileCache❬T❭ (74 lines); constructor     
  takes cacheName                                                                                                     
  • backend/src/extract-vibe/cache/extract-vibe-cache.service.ts — 10-line subclass; passes 'extract-vibe' to super() 
  • backend/src/outfit-avatar/cache/outfit-avatar-cache.service.ts — 45-line subclass; passes 'outfit-avatar' to      
  super() and adds buildKey() for SHA-256 key derivation                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  **Solution:** make JsonFileCache❬T❭ concrete and accept cacheName: string plus an optional keyFn callback. Replace  
  both subclasses with direct instantiation. Move buildKey logic to a small pure function co-located with the avatar  
  module. Delete the cache/ subdirectories.                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  **Wins:**                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  • Delete 2 shallow modules                                                                                          
  • One file to read instead of three when tracing a cache                                                            
  • buildKey becomes a testable pure function, not a method on a service                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • JsonFileCache❬T❭ in backend/src/common/cache/json-file-cache.service.ts is concrete (not abstract); constructor   
  accepts cacheName: string and an optional keyFn for custom key derivation                                           
  • backend/src/extract-vibe/cache/extract-vibe-cache.service.ts deleted; ExtractVibeModule provides                  
  JsonFileCache❬VibeProfile❭ directly                                                                                 
  • backend/src/outfit-avatar/cache/outfit-avatar-cache.service.ts deleted; buildKey logic extracted to a pure        
  function in backend/src/outfit-avatar/outfit-avatar.service.ts or a sibling helper file                             
  • OutfitAvatarModule provides JsonFileCache❬OutfitAvatarResponseDto❭ directly with the buildKey function wired as   
  keyFn                                                                                                               
  • Cache hit/miss behaviour identical post-refactor: same key derivation, same file paths under backend/data/cache/  
  • npm run build in backend/ passes with zero TS errors

  # Architecture: centralize the model registry                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sat, 27 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Model identity is split across inline string literals, env vars with defaults, and module-level constants — a       
  coordinated upgrade or A/B test requires edits in four files with no shared shape.                                  
                                                                                                                      
  **Files:**                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  • backend/src/extract-vibe/extract-vibe.service.ts — 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001' inline literal                     
  • backend/src/style-pieces/style-pieces.service.ts — 'claude-sonnet-4-6' inline literal                             
  • backend/src/outfit-avatar/outfit-avatar.service.ts — GOOGLE_IMAGE_MODEL read from ConfigService with an inline    
  default each call site                                                                                              
  • backend/src/embedding/embedding.service.ts — EMBEDDING_MODEL = 'text-embedding-3-small' module-level constant     
                                                                                                                      
  **Solution:** new backend/src/common/models/models.ts exporting a MODELS object with roles ( vibe , stylist , avatar
  , embed ). Each role defaults to the current model string and can be overridden by a corresponding env var. All four
  services consume MODELS.❬role❭ instead of their current scattered declarations.                                     
                                                                                                                      
  **Wins:**                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  • Model versions in one file                                                                                        
  • Coordinated upgrades become one edit                                                                              
  • Tests can inject a role to get a fake/cheap model                                                                 
  • Pairs naturally with KAN-89 (LlmCall) — MODELS becomes an input to the LlmCall interface                          
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • New backend/src/common/models/models.ts exporting {{MODELS = { vibe, stylist, avatar, embed }}} with string values
  matching current defaults                                                                                           
  • extract-vibe.service.ts uses MODELS.vibe instead of the inline 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001' literal                
  • style-pieces.service.ts uses MODELS.stylist instead of the inline 'claude-sonnet-4-6' literal                     
  • embedding.service.ts uses MODELS.embed instead of the file-local EMBEDDING_MODEL constant                         
  • outfit-avatar.service.ts uses MODELS.avatar instead of reading GOOGLE_IMAGE_MODEL from ConfigService inline       
  • Each role's env-var override documented in backend/.env.example                                                   
  • npm run build in backend/ passes with zero TS errors

⭐ Task  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 27 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-91  💭 0 comments  🧵 0 linked                          
                                                                                                                      
  # Architecture: make the four-stage pipeline a first-class module                                                   
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sat, 27 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  The four-stage pipeline shape (vibe → pieces → looks → avatars) is spread across Discover.jsx , runPipeline.js , and
  api.js . Skipping a stage, branching on regenerate, or inserting a progress event means editing all three files. The
  pipeline has no interface — it has a control flow.                                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  **Files:**                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  • frontend/src/screens/Discover/runPipeline.js — exports runExtractVibe , composeLooks , regenerateLook ; each      
  function is called ad-hoc from Discover.jsx                                                                         
  • frontend/src/screens/Discover/Discover.jsx — calls runExtractVibe (line 508) and composeLooks (line 526) directly;
  owns the onStage callback that threads through composeLooks                                                         
  • frontend/src/api.js — stage functions ( extractVibe , findPieces , stylePieces , regenerateOutfit ) imported      
  directly by runPipeline.js                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  **Solution:** create frontend/src/screens/Discover/pipeline.js exposing pipeline.run(input, { onStage }) and        
  pipeline.regenerate(opts) . Stages are data inside the module. composeLooks and regenerateLook move inside          
  pipeline.js . Discover.jsx calls pipeline.run(...) and pipeline.regenerate(...) only — no direct stage-function     
  imports.                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  **Wins:**                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  • Pipeline edits in one file                                                                                        
  • Regenerate flow reuses the same module as the main flow                                                           
  • Discover.jsx becomes input/render only                                                                            
  • composeLooks exported function disappears from runPipeline.js                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • New frontend/src/screens/Discover/pipeline.js with exported run(input, { onStage }) and regenerate(opts) functions
  • Discover.jsx imports run and regenerate from pipeline.js only — no direct imports of composeLooks , runExtractVibe ,
  or regenerateLook                                                                                                   
  • Stage progress events ( onStage('outfits') , onStage('avatars') ) emitted by the pipeline module, not managed in  
  Discover.jsx                                                                                                        
  • Regenerate flow calls pipeline.regenerate(...) with the same onStage contract as the main flow                    
  • npm run build in frontend/ passes with zero errors                                                                
  • Functional behaviour unchanged: celebrity-name-to-looks and regenerate-look flows produce identical API call      
  sequences

⭐ Task  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 27 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-90  💭 0 comments  🧵 1 linked                          
                                                                                                                      
  # Architecture: unify CatalogItem — three shapes, one concept                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sat, 27 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  CatalogItem is declared in two places under the same name, forcing find-pieces to import both and alias one to      
  DbCatalogItem . A toCatalogItem() mapper exists only to shed the similarity field. The seam between persistence     
  shape and API shape is doing nothing.                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  **Files:**                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  • backend/src/catalog/catalog-search.service.ts — owns CatalogItem (DB shape with similarity?: number )             
  • backend/src/types/catalog.ts — declares a second CatalogItem (API shape with name , buy_url , price_tier ,        
  style_tags , colors )                                                                                               
  • backend/src/find-pieces/find-pieces.service.ts — imports CatalogItem as DbCatalogItem from the catalog module and 
  CatalogItem from types; maps via toCatalogItem() to shed similarity                                                 
  • backend/src/style-pieces/style-pieces.dto.ts — CatalogItemDto is a third declaration of the same concept          
                                                                                                                      
  **Solution:** make catalog-search.service.ts 's CatalogItem the canonical shape — add name , buy_url , price_tier , 
  style_tags , colors as optional fields alongside the existing DB fields. Delete backend/src/types/catalog.ts .      
  Remove the DbCatalogItem alias and toCatalogItem() from FindPiecesService . Align CatalogItemDto to the canonical   
  type.                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  **Context:** best tackled after KAN-89 (LlmCall) so that style-pieces has already been touched once; avoids a       
  concurrent edit conflict on the same service.                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • CatalogItem exported from backend/src/catalog/catalog-search.service.ts only; backend/src/types/catalog.ts deleted
  • DbCatalogItem alias removed from find-pieces.service.ts ; it imports CatalogItem from the catalog module with no  
  renaming                                                                                                            
  • toCatalogItem() method removed from FindPiecesService                                                             
  • CatalogItemDto in style-pieces.dto.ts references the canonical CatalogItem shape (or is eliminated in favour of   
  it) — no third independent declaration                                                                              
  • npm run build in backend/ passes with zero TS errors                                                              
  • Existing /find-pieces response shape is unchanged at runtime (same field names and values)                        
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  IS BLOCKED BY                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  KAN-89 Architecture: collapse LLM-call boilerplate into one deep module • Task • Medium • Idea

⭐ Task  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 27 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-89  💭 0 comments  🧵 1 linked                          
                                                                                                                      
  # Architecture: collapse LLM-call boilerplate into one deep module                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sat, 27 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Collapse the repeated LLM-ceremony boilerplate (retry loop, transient-error matching, sleep+jitter, tool-result     
  extraction, safe-parse) that currently lives independently in three pipeline services into a single LlmCall module. 
                                                                                                                      
  **Files:**                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  • backend/src/extract-vibe/extract-vibe.service.ts — calls ToolRunner with model hardcoded as 'claude-haiku-4-5-    
  20251001'                                                                                                           
  • backend/src/style-pieces/style-pieces.service.ts — model hardcoded as 'claude-sonnet-4-6' ; JSON.parse(rawOutfits)
  workaround for array coercion                                                                                       
  • backend/src/outfit-avatar/outfit-avatar.service.ts — duplicates retry + sleep + transient-error detection for     
  Gemini calls (independent from the Anthropic path)                                                                  
  • backend/src/common/anthropic/anthropic-tool-runner.ts — Anthropic-specific retry/sleep, consumed by extract-vibe  
  and style-pieces but not outfit-avatar                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  **Solution:** one LlmCall module at backend/src/common/llm/ behind .run({ tool, model, input, schema }) . Tool      
  schemas become injectable data. Retry policy, transient-error map, schema validation, and safe-parse live inside.   
  Gemini retry in outfit-avatar follows the same pattern once unified.                                                
                                                                                                                      
  **Wins:**                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  • Retry policy in one place                                                                                         
  • JSON.parse(rawOutfits) workaround in style-pieces.service.ts disappears — schema coercion handles it              
  • Tool schemas testable as plain data, not service-internal constants                                               
  • One interface consumed by all Anthropic call sites; anthropic-tool-runner.ts becomes an implementation detail, not
  a public API                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • New module at backend/src/common/llm/llm-call.service.ts with .run({ tool, model, input, schema }) interface      
  • extract-vibe.service.ts and style-pieces.service.ts reduced to llmCall.run(...) — no retry boilerplate in service 
  body                                                                                                                
  • JSON.parse(rawOutfits) workaround in style-pieces.service.ts removed; schema validates and coerces the array      
  response                                                                                                            
  • Retry policy, transient-error matching, and sleep+jitter live only in LlmCall ; duplicated copies in anthropic-   
  tool-runner.ts eliminated                                                                                           
  • Unit tests in backend/test/unit/llm-call.service.spec.ts cover: transient error triggers retry, max retries       
  exceeded throws, schema mismatch throws                                                                             
  • npm run build in backend/ passes with zero TS errors after the refactor                                           
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------

  # Styling benchmark harness — fixture-driven side-by-side vs baseline                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sat, 27 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Fixture-driven benchmark harness that runs the new rubric pipeline (KAN-76 → KAN-77 → KAN-79 → KAN-80) against the  
  current single-Sonnet baseline on the same inputs and emits a side-by-side report. Used to validate quality lift    
  before                                                                                                              
  shipping and as a regression net after.h2. Scope                                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  **Script location**                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  • backend/scripts/styling-benchmark.ts (mjs/ts — match existing scripts/)                                           
  • npm script: bench:styling in backend/package.json                                                                 
  • Run: npm run --prefix backend bench:styling [-- --fixtures=path --output=path]                                    
                                                                                                                      
  **Fixture format (backend/test/fixtures/styling/)**                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  • 10 representative cases at MVP, expandable                                                                        
  • Each fixture: { name, user_profile, vibe, occasion, items, expected_signals? }                                    
  • Diversity across: 5 body shapes × 3 occasions (editorial / commercial / personal) × 3 vibe families (minimalist / 
  maximalist / streetwear)                                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  **What the benchmark runs**                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  For each fixture:                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                      
  1. Run baseline (current single-Sonnet /style-pieces)                                                               
  2. Run rubric pipeline (Plan → Compose K=6 → Critique → Select 3)                                                   
  3. Score both outputs along deterministic rubric checks:                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  • has_wearable_core (top+bottom OR dress)                                                                           
  • hero_role_assigned                                                                                                
  • hero_in_top_or_dress_category                                                                                     
  • volume_rule_respected (against StylingPlan.volume_zone)                                                           
  • anchor_neutral_present_in_outfit                                                                                  
  • texture_score_meets_threshold (where available)                                                                   
  • focal_point_count_equals_1 (where available)                                                                      
  • rationale_per_piece_complete (every item has satisfies field)                                                     
  • alternates_have_distinct_hero_pieces                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  4. Token + latency:                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  • tokens spent per stage (plan, compose, critic, select)                                                            
  • wall-clock per stage                                                                                              
  • total cost in USD using the Anthropic pricing table                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  **Report output**                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                      
  • /tmp/styling-benchmark-/report.md with:                                                                           
  • Per-fixture diff: baseline outfit IDs vs rubric outfit IDs                                                        
  • Rubric compliance table (baseline % vs rubric %)                                                                  
  • Cost + latency rollup (baseline total vs rubric total, p50/p95 per stage)                                         
  • /tmp/styling-benchmark-/raw.jsonl with full pipeline outputs for grepping                                         
                                                                                                                      
  **Baseline-only mode**                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Flag --baseline-only runs only the current pipeline and emits compliance scores                                   
  • Lets this ticket land BEFORE KAN-76..KAN-80, providing the starting line measurement                              
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Runs end-to-end against local DB without flakiness on the 10 fixtures                                             
  • Report is human-readable AND machine-readable (jsonl)                                                             
  • Reports cost-per-look in USD per pipeline                                                                         
  • --baseline-only flag works before rubric pipeline ships                                                           
  • README in backend/scripts/ explaining how to add fixtures                                                         
  • No CI integration required at MVP — local tool                                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Depends on                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  • Can start in baseline-only mode immediately                                                                       
  • Full functionality unblocks after KAN-80                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  ## References                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  • second-brain/fashion-ai/wiki/styling/ (rubric definition)                                                         
  • airflow/scripts/benchmark.py (similar pattern in scraper-qa)

⭐ Task  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 27 Jun 26  👷 Jae Yoon Bae  🔑️ KAN-80  💭 0 comments  🧵 2 linked                        
                                                                                                                      
  # Selection + diversity ranking — pick primary + 2 alternates from scored candidates                                
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sat, 27 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Pick the final primary look + 2 alternates from the K=6 scored candidates emerging from KAN-79, enforcing the       
  rubric's "meaningfully different premise" diversity rule. Wire the result into the existing /style-pieces response  
  shape (backwards-compatible).h2. Scope                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  **Input**                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  • K scored candidates from KAN-79 (each with texture_score, focal_point_count, hierarchy_conflicts, roles,          
  rationale)                                                                                                          
  • StylingPlan for context (palette_type, silhouette decision)                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  **Selection algorithm**                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  1. Drop candidates with passes_filter === false                                                                     
  2. If 0 survive: fall back per rubric — re-prompt the composer with reject_reasons, OR (cheaper) return the highest-
  scoring rejected candidate with a flag in rationale                                                                 
  3. Rank survivors by composite score: texture_score weighted 0.5, focal-point cleanliness 0.3, hierarchy_conflicts. 
  length 0.2                                                                                                          
  4. Pick the top-scored candidate as primary                                                                         
  5. For each of the 2 alternates: scan remaining survivors for the highest score whose hero piece differs from the   
  primary AND from previously picked alternates (per rubric Step 8)                                                   
  6. If fewer than 2 hero-distinct candidates remain, surface alternates_flag: "Insufficient piece variety to generate
  distinct alternates" (per Step 8)                                                                                   
                                                                                                                      
  **Output → /style-pieces response shape**                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  • outfits: 3 ResolvedOutfit entries with full structured rationale (new field) AND why populated from rationale.    
  concept_alignment (back-compat)                                                                                     
  • New optional field on response: plan_summary — surface the StylingPlan decisions so the frontend can show "styled 
  for analogous warm palette, A-line silhouette, broad-shoulder balance"                                              
                                                                                                                      
  **Regenerate path**                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  • /style-pieces/regenerate inherits the same pipeline: PlanService → ComposerService(K=6) → CriticService →         
  SelectionService picks 1 instead of 3                                                                               
  • Diversity constraint relaxes to "hero piece differs from prior_outfit"                                            
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Always returns exactly the requested count when ≥ count candidates survive

  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Pick the final primary look + 2 alternates from the K=6 scored candidates emerging from KAN-79, enforcing the       
  rubric's "meaningfully different premise" diversity rule. Wire the result into the existing /style-pieces response  
  shape (backwards-compatible).h2. Scope

  **Selection algorithm**                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  1. Drop candidates with passes_filter === false                                                                     
  2. If 0 survive: fall back per rubric — re-prompt the composer with reject_reasons, OR (cheaper) return the highest-
  scoring rejected candidate with a flag in rationale                                                                 
  3. Rank survivors by composite score: texture_score weighted 0.5, focal-point cleanliness 0.3, hierarchy_conflicts. 
  length 0.2                                                                                                          
  4. Pick the top-scored candidate as primary                                                                         
  5. For each of the 2 alternates: scan remaining survivors for the highest score whose hero piece differs from the   
  primary AND from previously picked alternates (per rubric Step 8)                                                   
  6. If fewer than 2 hero-distinct candidates remain, surface alternates_flag: "Insufficient piece variety to generate
  distinct alternates" (per Step 8)                                                                                   
                                                                                                                      
  **Output → /style-pieces response shape**                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  • outfits: 3 ResolvedOutfit entries with full structured rationale (new field) AND why populated from rationale.    
  concept_alignment (back-compat)                                                                                     
  • New optional field on response: plan_summary — surface the StylingPlan decisions so the frontend can show "styled 
  for analogous warm palette, A-line silhouette, broad-shoulder balance"

  # StylingCriticService — texture, focal-point, and hierarchy scoring per candidate                                  
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sat, 27 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  New backend/src/styling-critic/ module that scores each of the K=6 candidates from KAN-77 along the rubric's quality
  dimensions and applies the deterministic threshold filters. Runs in parallel across candidates.h2. Scope            
                                                                                                                      
  **Inputs (per candidate)**                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  • Resolved outfit items (with their catalog description field — already rich Sonnet-generated silhouette/fabric     
  prose)                                                                                                              
  • Assigned roles { hero, supporting, accessory } from KAN-77                                                        
  • StylingPlan from KAN-76 (for editorial override context)                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  **Outputs (per candidate)**                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  • texture_score (0–100)                                                                                             
  • texture_dimensions: surface_contrast (0–40), pattern_discipline (0–30), weight_coherence (0–20), seasonal_hand    
  (0–10)                                                                                                              
  • focal_point_count + focal_points[] with reasons                                                                   
  • hierarchy_conflicts[]                                                                                             
  • passes_filter + reject_reasons[]                                                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  **Implementation**                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  • Texture scoring: one Haiku call per candidate that reads the item descriptions and returns the 4 dimension scores 
  per rubric Step 4                                                                                                   
  • Focal-point audit: one Haiku call per candidate counting independent focal elements per rubric Step 5             
  • Hierarchy check: pure-TS deterministic against roles (hero category, supporting non-competing, accessory          
  subordinate) per Step 6                                                                                             
  • All K candidates scored in parallel (Promise.all)                                                                 
  • Reuse ToolRunner; Haiku model                                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  **Threshold filter**                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  • texture_score ❬ 60 → reject (or ❬ 50 if concept_authority === HIGH)                                               
  • focal_point_count ≠ 1 → reject (or = 2 allowed if override_flag === true and concept brief calls for focal        
  tension)                                                                                                            
  • hierarchy_conflicts.length ❭ 0 → reject                                                                           
  • All rejects surface a reject_reason; the selector (KAN-80) decides what to do if all 6 reject                     
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • New module at backend/src/styling-critic/ with service + types                                                    
  • Unit tests for hierarchy deterministic checks                                                                     
  • Integration-style tests that mock Haiku responses for texture + focal scoring                                     
  • Editorial override pass-through tested (HIGH authority allows lower texture threshold + focal=2)                  
  • Parallel execution confirmed (no serial Haiku calls)                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ## Depends on                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  • KAN-77 (composer producing K=6 candidates with roles)

  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Restructure backend/src/style-pieces/style-pieces.service.ts so it consumes a StylingPlan (from KAN-76) and returns 
  per-                                                                                                                
  piece structured rationale + assigned roles. Overgenerate K=6 candidates internally so the critic (KAN-79) and      
  selector (KAN-80) have something to score and rank.                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  ## Scope                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  **Prompt rewrite (**  backend/src/style-pieces/style-pieces.service.ts  **)**

  StylingPlan

  Extend STYLE_PIECES_TOOL.input_schema.properties.outfits.items with:                                                
                                                                                                                      
  • roles : object with hero (piece_id string), supporting (string[]), accessory (string[]) — hero + supporting       
  required                                                                                                            
  • rationale : object with concept_alignment (string), per_piece (array of {{{ piece_id, role, satisfies, tension?,  
  corrects? }}}), flags (string[])                                                                                    
  **Overgeneration**                                                                                                  
  • Internal count raised to K=6 (configurable constant)                                                              
  • API request count still respected for response shape; KAN-80 picks the final 3 from the K=6                       
  **Backwards compatibility**                                                                                         
  • Existing OutfitDto.why is populated from rationale.concept_alignment (❬=180 chars)                                
  • New OutfitDto.rationale field is additive — old clients ignore it                                                 
  • Update Swagger DTOs and frontend types

  • Prompt is built from StylingPlan , not raw vibe + occasion                                                        
  • Tool schema enforces roles + structured rationale on every outfit                                                 
  • Returns 6 candidates internally; KAN-80 trims to 3                                                                
  • All existing /style-pieces e2e tests pass (with why populated from new path)

  • Swagger docs reflect new response shape

⭐ Epic  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 27 Jun 26  👷 Jae Yoon Bae  🔑️ KAN-75  💭 0 comments  🧵 0 linked                        
                                                                                                                      
  # Apply 9-step styling rubric to outfit composition                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sat, 27 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Replace the single-Sonnet flat prompt in /style-pieces with a structured Plan → Compose → Critique → Select pipeline
  that applies the 9-step styling rubric documented in second-brain/fashion-ai/wiki/styling .                         
                                                                                                                      
  ## Why                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  The current composer ( backend/src/style-pieces/style-pieces.service.ts ) is one Claude Sonnet call with a flat vibe +
  occasion + items prompt. It has no body-shape awareness, no explicit palette architecture, no focal-point or texture
  scoring, and only a 180-char rationale. The rubric formalises how a trained stylist composes: body data → silhouette
  rules, vibe + undertone → palette architecture, occasion → concept authority, then per-candidate scoring on texture 
  and focal point, then per-piece structured rationale.                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ## Implementation tickets                                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
   **Key**                                                | **Summary**                                               
  --------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------  
   KAN-76                                                 | StylingPlanService — deterministic rules engine for       
                                                          | silhouette, palette, concept authority                    
   KAN-77                                                 | Rubric composer rewrite — /style-pieces prompt +          
                                                          | structured rationale schema                               
   KAN-79                                                 | StylingCriticService — texture, focal-point, and          
                                                          | hierarchy scoring per candidate                           
   KAN-80                                                 | Selection + diversity ranking — pick primary + 2          
                                                          | alternates from scored candidates                         
   KAN-81                                                 | Styling benchmark harness — fixture-driven side-by-side   
                                                          | vs baseline                                               
   Ship order: KAN-76 → KAN-77 → KAN-79 → KAN-80 → KAN-   |                                                           
   81. KAN-81 can run in --baseline-only mode before the  |                                                           
   others land.                                           |                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  ## Rubric reference                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  Full pipeline + rationale at second-brain/fashion-ai/wiki/styling/agent-pipeline.md . Steps 0–8, each with required 
  inputs and produced outputs.

⭐ Task  🚧 In Progress  ⌛ Fri, 26 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-74  💭 0 comments  🧵 0 linked                   
                                                                                                                      
  # Retrieval bake-off in find-pieces: current vs HyDE vs HyDE+rerank                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 26 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  ## Why                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  Stage 2 ( find-pieces ) is the floor on outfit quality — Stage 3 styling can only ever be as good as the candidates 
  it gets. Today the retrieval query is sparse ( anchor + aesthetic_tags + color_palette[].name ) and embedded once   
  with text-embedding-3-small , then a per-category pgvector cosine search. The query distribution is "tags + color   
  words"; the catalog embeddings are dense product *descriptions* generated by Sonnet at ingest. That mismatch likely 
  costs us a lot of relevance.                                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  Before investing in productionizing any one fix, we want to prove which retrieval strategy actually wins on our     
  catalog and our vibes.                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ## What — three strategies in parallel                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  Implement A / B / C side-by-side behind a flag in backend/src/find-pieces/ :                                        
                                                                                                                      
  • **A (control):** current behavior — query string from anchor + tags + color names, one embedding, one cosine      
  search per category.                                                                                                
  • **B (HyDE):** for each category (top/bottom/dress/shoes/accessory), a small Sonnet/Haiku call writes the *ideal   
  product description* in the same prose voice as catalog descriptions (e.g. "oversized cropped wool blazer in oat,   
  structured shoulder, hip-length, mother-of-pearl buttons"). Embed that, search.                                     
  • **C (HyDE + rerank):** same as B, but pull top 30 per category from the vector search and run a Haiku reranker    
  pass that scores each candidate against the full VibeProfile and returns the top 5 with a one-line rationale.       
  All three strategies run against the same CatalogSearchService and the same gender/price filters — only the query   
  construction (and optional rerank step) differs.                                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Eval setup                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  • Pick ~20 representative vibes covering the kinds of inputs we've seen (mix of celebrity-only, celebrity+photo,    
  different aesthetics, both genders).                                                                                
  • For each vibe, capture the top 5 items per category from each of A / B / C.                                       
  • Score two ways:                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                      
  1. **LLM judge:** a Claude Sonnet call sees the VibeProfile and the 5 items (descriptions + image URLs); returns a  
  1–10 score per category + a one-line rationale + a flagged-issue list. Run per (vibe × strategy × category).        
  2. **Human spot-check:** eyeball ~5 of the 20 vibes end-to-end to sanity-check that the judge isn't gaming or       
  systematically biased.                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Aggregate: mean judge score per strategy, per-category breakdown, and win-rate (which strategy wins per vibe).    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Deliverable                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  • The three strategies behind a flag in find-pieces (env var or request-level param — engineer's call, but it must  
  not change default user-facing behavior).                                                                           
  • A small report committed to the repo (markdown or a static HTML page under backend/data/ or similar) with: per-   
  vibe winners, aggregate scores, per-category breakdown, and 3–5 example side-by-side comparisons (vibe → A items vs 
  B items vs C items).                                                                                                
  • A short written recommendation: which strategy to productionize, and what the next retrieval ticket should be.    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Out of scope                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  • Image-side / CLIP retrieval (separate follow-up if B or C wins decisively).                                       
  • Outfit-aware retrieval (picking bottoms conditioned on the chosen top) — separate follow-up.                      
  • Multi-query expansion beyond the single HyDE description per category — separate follow-up.                       
  • Changing the default user-facing retrieval path. This ticket only adds the experimental code paths and produces   
  the report.                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  ## Success criteria                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  We can point at a number and say "strategy X beats current by Y points on average, here are the side-by-sides,      
  here's                                                                                                              
  the next ticket." Even a null result (current is fine) is a valid outcome — it just redirects effort to Stage 3.

  ------------------------ Description ------------------------

  ownership at a glance.

  ## Out of scope                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  • No schema changes.                                                                                                
  • No new migrations.

  # FE: PostHog initialization and event tracking                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Thu, 25 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Install and initialize PostHog in the frontend so that user-flow events reach the PostHog analytics dashboard.h2.   
  Context                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  PostHog is not yet initialized in the frontend — no posthog-js package exists and no posthog.capture() calls are    
  present. This instrumentation is required before the A/B paywall experiment (KAN-53) can be configured.             
                                                                                                                      
  ## Changes                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  • frontend/package.json — add posthog-js dependency                                                                 
  • frontend/src/main.jsx — initialize PostHog with VITE_POSTHOG_KEY and VITE_POSTHOG_HOST env vars; skip             
  initialization when the key is absent (local dev / CI)                                                              
  • frontend/src/analytics.js (new) — thin track(event, props) wrapper so screens don't import PostHog directly       
  • **Individual screens** — call track() at the moments listed below                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  ## Events to instrument                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
   **Event**                                               | **Location**                                             
  ---------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------  
   pipeline_started                                        | Discover.jsx — user submits the vibe form                
   vibe_extracted                                          | Discover.jsx — vibe profile returned                     
   outfits_styled                                          | Discover.jsx — outfit cards rendered                     
   outfit_saved                                            | Wardrobe screen / save action                            
   paywall_viewed                                          | Paywall.jsx — on mount                                   
   checkout_started                                        | Paywall.jsx — user clicks "unlock my closet"             
   signup_completed                                        | Auth/Signup.jsx — registration succeeds                  
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • posthog-js added to frontend/package.json                                                                         
  • PostHog initialized in main.jsx ; no error thrown when VITE_POSTHOG_KEY is absent                                 
  • All events in the table above fire at the correct moments in the running app                                      
  • npm run build in frontend/ passes                                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  BLOCKS                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  KAN-53 A/B test paywall variants via PostHog feature flags • Task • Medium • Done KAN-51 Analytics: PostHog         
  instrumentation (FE + BE)        • Task • Medium • Testing

  # Onboarding: brand picker grouped by category                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  frontend, sprint-1  👀 You are watching                
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Update frontend/src/screens/Onboarding/BrandSelection.jsx so brands are rendered under fashion-category headers (e.g.
  Streetwear, Y2K, Old money, Minimal) instead of a flat alphabetical list.                                           
                                                                                                                      
  The brand list comes from GET /brands (backed by backend/src/brands/brands.service.ts which reads distinct brands   
  from catalog_items ). Category membership must be defined in a new static config file — e.g.                        
  frontend/src/screens/Onboarding/brand-categories.js — since the catalog DB has no fashion-category column on brands.
                                                                                                                      
  User selections still persist via updateMe({ preferred_brands: [...] }) to User.preferred_brands (already a String[]
  column on the users table).                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Brands are rendered under labelled category headers; users can select/deselect any brand across all categories    
  • Selections save correctly via updateMe and are returned in subsequent GET /me responses                           
  • Category membership is defined in a clearly named static config file, not inline in JSX                           
  • npm run build (in frontend/ ) passes                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ## Needs human input                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Which brands from the catalog belong to which fashion category (Streetwear, Y2K, Old money, Minimal, etc.)? A   
  suggested mapping from the product owner is needed to seed the static config — or should the developer make a best- 
  effort grouping and get sign-off in the PR review?
