input.content:
Replace the single-Sonnet flat prompt in /style-pieces with a structured Plan → Compose → Critique → Select pipeline that applies the 9-step styling rubric documented in second-brain/fashion-ai/wiki/styling .

## Why

The current composer ( backend/src/style-pieces/style-pieces.service.ts ) is one Claude Sonnet call with a flat vibe + occasion + items prompt. It has no body-shape awareness, no explicit palette architecture, no focal-point or texture scoring, and only a 180-char rationale. The rubric formalises how a trained stylist composes: body data → silhouette rules, vibe + undertone → palette architecture, occasion → concept authority, then per-candidate scoring on texture and focal point, then per-piece structured rationale.

## Implementation tickets

| Key    | Summary |
|--------|---------|
| KAN-76 | StylingPlanService — deterministic rules engine for silhouette, palette, concept authority |
| KAN-77 | Rubric composer rewrite — /style-pieces prompt + structured rationale schema |
| KAN-79 | StylingCriticService — texture, focal-point, and hierarchy scoring per candidate |
| KAN-80 | Selection + diversity ranking — pick primary + 2 alternates from scored candidates |
| KAN-81 | Styling benchmark harness — fixture-driven side-by-side vs baseline |

Ship order: KAN-76 → KAN-77 → KAN-79 → KAN-80 → KAN-81. KAN-81 can run in --baseline-only mode before the others land.

## Rubric reference

Full pipeline + rationale at second-brain/fashion-ai/wiki/styling/agent-pipeline.md . Steps 0–8, each with required inputs and produced outputs.

## Acceptance criteria

- All child implementation tickets (KAN-77, KAN-79, KAN-80, KAN-81) shipped and merged
- /style-pieces endpoint runs the full Plan → Compose (K=6) → Critique → Select pipeline end-to-end
- Benchmark (KAN-81) committed with rubric compliance scores for the new pipeline vs single-Sonnet baseline
- Existing /style-pieces response shape unchanged for callers that don't consume the new rationale or plan_summary fields

input.content:
## Why

Stage 2 ( find-pieces ) is the floor on outfit quality — Stage 3 styling can only ever be as good as the candidates it gets. Today the retrieval query is sparse ( anchor + aesthetic_tags + color_palette[].name ) and embedded once with text-embedding-3-small , then a per-category pgvector cosine search. The query distribution is "tags + color words"; the catalog embeddings are dense product descriptions generated by Sonnet at ingest. That mismatch likely costs us a lot of relevance.

Before investing in productionizing any one fix, we want to prove which retrieval strategy actually wins on our catalog and our vibes.

## What — three strategies in parallel

Implement A / B / C side-by-side behind a flag in backend/src/find-pieces/ :

- **A (control):** current behavior — query string from anchor + tags + color names, one embedding, one cosine search per category.
- **B (HyDE):** for each category (top/bottom/dress/shoes/accessory), a small Sonnet/Haiku call writes the ideal product description in the same prose voice as catalog descriptions (e.g. "oversized cropped wool blazer in oat, structured shoulder, hip-length, mother-of-pearl buttons"). Embed that, search.
- **C (HyDE + rerank):** same as B, but pull top 30 per category from the vector search and run a Haiku reranker pass that scores each candidate against the full VibeProfile and returns the top 5 with a one-line rationale.

All three strategies run against the same CatalogSearchService and the same gender/price filters — only the query construction (and optional rerank step) differs.

## Eval setup

- Pick ~20 representative vibes covering the kinds of inputs we've seen (mix of celebrity-only, celebrity+photo, different aesthetics, both genders).
- For each vibe, capture the top 5 items per category from each of A / B / C.
- Score two ways:
  1. **LLM judge:** a Claude Sonnet call sees the VibeProfile and the 5 items (descriptions + image URLs); returns a 1–10 score per category + a one-line rationale + a flagged-issue list. Run per (vibe × strategy × category).
  2. **Human spot-check:** eyeball ~5 of the 20 vibes end-to-end to sanity-check that the judge isn't gaming or systematically biased.
- Aggregate: mean judge score per strategy, per-category breakdown, and win-rate (which strategy wins per vibe).

## Deliverable

- The three strategies behind a flag in find-pieces (env var or request-level param — engineer's call, but it must not change default user-facing behavior).
- A small report committed to the repo (markdown or a static HTML page under backend/data/ or similar) with: per-vibe winners, aggregate scores, per-category breakdown, and 3–5 example side-by-side comparisons (vibe → A items vs B items vs C items).
- A short written recommendation: which strategy to productionize, and what the next retrieval ticket should be.

## Out of scope

- Image-side / CLIP retrieval (separate follow-up if B or C wins decisively).
- Outfit-aware retrieval (picking bottoms conditioned on the chosen top) — separate follow-up.
- Multi-query expansion beyond the single HyDE description per category — separate follow-up.
- Changing the default user-facing retrieval path. This ticket only adds the experimental code paths and produces the report.

## Acceptance criteria

- Strategies A, B, and C implemented in backend/src/find-pieces/ behind a flag; default user-facing behavior unchanged
- All three strategies query the same CatalogSearchService with the same gender/price filters
- Eval runs against ~20 representative vibes with LLM judge scores (1–10 per strategy per category)
- Report committed under backend/data/ (or similar) in markdown; includes per-vibe winners, aggregate scores, per-category breakdown, and ≥3 side-by-side comparisons
- Written recommendation committed with the report identifying which strategy to productionize and suggesting the next retrieval ticket
